rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc???S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determining Validity Of Third Party Claims (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DETERMINE VALIDITY OF THIRD-PARTY CLAIMS (BY RESHMA KAMATH O/B/O NEW OWNERS GROUP LLC, ET AL) Hearing Required. Each third-party claimant must appear in-person at the hearing. If the claimant is an entity, a principal must appear. For any trusts or estates, a trustee or an administrator must appear. All counsel shall also appear in person. If claimants intend to produce any evidence at the hearing, claimants must have two copies of all documents to be provided to the Court at 9:00 a.m. and to Plaintiff's counsel. Any evidence that is not documentary evidence (e.g. Purchase and Sale Agreement, Grant Deed, etc) must be submitted in a form of a declaration and courtesy copies delivered to the Court no later than 9:00 a.m. on January 3, 2024. The Court will consider all competent admissible evidence filed and delivered to department 501 by 9:00 a.m. on January 3, 2024. Upon review of the documents on file with the Court at the time of the issuance of this tentative ruling, the Court is inclined to find the claims invalid. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMESTOWN SA LLC; ET AL VS. JONATHAN DISHOTSKY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO OLASCOAGA DBA PACIFIC GREEN CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is moot. The Fourth Amended Complaint is stricken on the Court's own motion. On October 25, 2023 Plaintiff was ordered to prepare an order reflecting the Court's ruling, which, inter alia, allowed the filing of the amended complaint and continued the trial. Plaintiff's failure to provide a proposed order for over two months after the hearing, resulted in the trial date as of today's date remain on January 8, 2024. This is not consistent with the Court's October 25, 2023 ruling, therefore, the Fourth Amended Complaint filed prematurely before the order reflecting the entirety of the Court's ruling was entered, is hereby stricken. The matter shall proceed to trial on January 8, 2024 based on the pleadings on the Third Amended Complaint. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION, AN VS. H2A ENTERTAINMENT, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT H2A ENTERTAINMENT LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS is continued to January 19, 2024. No later than January 5, 2024 Plaintiff shall file and serve written discovery on the defendants covering the "number of employees" issue. No later than January 11, 2023 by 5:00 p.m. Defendant to provide complete verified responses (and produce documents if discovery requests include request for production) electronically or by hand-delivery. No later than January 16, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to whether the subpoenaed documents are still being sought in light of the responses received. The Court will address the Bank of America subpoena at the continued hearing. No later than January 4, 2024 by 5:00 p.m. Defendant to file a supplemental brief addressing its standing to bring a motion to quash/modify on behalf of Rooftop Hospitality LLC. In the alternative, no later than January 11, 2024 Rooftop Hospitality LLC may file its own motion and calendar it for January 19, 2024. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GHNAIM PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHELLEY WIENKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT SHELLEY WIENKE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS No later than 9:00 a.m. on January 3, 2023 Plaintiff to file an amended proof of service including a due diligence declaration. If such amended proof of service is timely filed, the motion will be denied and Defendant will be ordered to respond within 5 days of notice of entry of order. If no amended proof of service accompanied by a due diligence declaration as to the moving defendant is on file as of 9:00 a.m. on January 3, 2024, the motion will be granted as Plaintiff presented no evidence that substitute service was resorted to after attempts had been made at personal service. The Court notes that declarations filed in opposition to this motion are not signed (courtesy copies contain signature); and neither the proof of service on file nor the proof of service attached to the unsigned declarations on file contains a due diligence declaration as to Shelley Wienke. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
